title: WiFi-Radar-for-SLAM: Ambient WiFi as a Radar Replacement for Automotive SLAM
description: A physics-based (Sionna RT ray-traced) feasibility study and system for using ambient sub-7 GHz WiFi signals received on a moving vehicle to build a 3D scan of the surroundings usable in place of radar in SLAM pipelines. Vehicle localization is centimetre-level and, with joint 2-D (delay-angle) MUSIC, reaches oracle quality from realistic commodity CSI in sparse-multipath scenes. Mapping is two-tier: ~25-30 cm with clean single-bounce (oracle) sensing and ~4-5 m with realistic CSI. A 60 GHz (1.76 GHz bandwidth) and a 16-antenna test show this mapping floor is removed by neither wider bandwidth nor larger aperture: it is a path-discrimination limit of commodity CSI, not a resolution limit, identifying the key open problem for WiFi mapping while localization stays practical.
